sources.notes: Game over arrays a,b for exactly k turns; final score is the maximum non-empty subarray sum.
Official editorial reduces paired moves by parity and computes best subarray passing through each modified index.
Shayan's solution discussion was used as an additional reference pointer for this round.

annotation.summary: Alice and Bob alternately add or subtract b_i from some a_i, then the score is the maximum non-empty subarray sum.
annotation.constraints: Total n is 2e5, so only linear scans over the array are allowed.
annotation.core_idea: Moves on the same index can cancel in pairs, so only the parity of k matters. If k is even, the original maximum subarray sum is forced. If k is odd, Alice gets one effective last change and should choose an index i; the best subarray affected by this change is the best suffix ending at i plus the best prefix starting at i minus a_i, then plus b_i.
tricks: best subarray through a chosen index

solution_variants.name: parity-kadane
solution_variants.summary: Reduce the game to zero or one effective move, then use left/right Kadane values to test every possible changed index.

tags.evidence: The game is solved by the optimal cancellation strategy between Alice and Bob, leaving only k mod 2 effective moves.
The final value is computed by Kadane-style maximum subarray DP from both directions.
tags.description: Kadane-style dynamic programming for maximum subarray sums, including directional best prefix/suffix variants.
tags.created_reason: Maximum subarray DP is a reusable retrieval category distinct from generic DP and prefix maximum optimization.

tags.evidence: After the cancellation reduction, Alice greedily chooses the single index whose modified through-subarray value is largest.
